commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ce11c92785fde3a3e53d1fd4d574efa4dfd07d5f)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Feishu bot-sender detection, bot-name lookup, per-chat mention registry state, outbound mention normalization, dispatcher reply-routing changes, and Feishu tests for bot-to-bot `@mention` delivery.

PR surface: Source +1025, Tests +1212. Total +2237 across 20 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the reported gap at source/proof level: the PR body gives live Feishu evidence that dispatcher replies now work and separately reproduces the message_tool silent-drop path. I did not run a live Feishu setup in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - The remaining blocker is an owner/product decision about whether dispatcher-only Feishu bot-to-bot support may land while message_tool remains a known follow-up.

target.title: feat(feishu): bot-to-bot conversation support with @mention handling
target.body:
## Summary

Lets the Feishu agent take part in bot-to-bot conversations and makes outbound `@mentions` correct and robust. In a Feishu group a message only notifies the entities it explicitly `@mentions`, so a bot replying to another bot without an `@` silently fails — the other bot never receives it and the conversation dies.

- Treat incoming bot messages like user messages so the agent can take part in bot-to-bot threads.
- New `bot-name` resolution module backed by the bot batch API as a weak dependency: falls back to the `open_id` when the bot info scope is absent.
- Per-chat mention registry resolving display name → `open_id` for outbound use.
- Outbound mention handling: teach `<at user_id="...">` syntax in the system prompt, strip only the leading self-mention, preserve + dedup other mentions.
- Guarantee bot-to-bot replies `@mention` the bot sender (deterministic `ensureMention` backstop on the dispatcher path) and deliver inline instead of in a topic thread.
- Core owns the group reply-delivery mode; the feishu `@mention` hint stays delivery-mode-agnostic (no duplicate delivery-mode computation in the plugin).

Aligns with #71396 — main no longer auto-prepends mentions, so this does not reintroduce the third-party mention cascade: the `mentionTargets` block stays context-only and never tells the model to re-`@` listed users.

Scope: 20 files, all under `extensions/feishu/` (+2278 / -41). No core changes.

## Verification

- `pnpm test extensions/feishu` — green (920+ tests pass), including the new `outbound-mention`, `mention-registry`, `bot-name`, and `bot.*` suites.
- Real-machine bot-to-bot session in a live Feishu group (see proof below).

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: a group bot-to-bot reply now `@mentions` the bot sender, so the other bot actually receives it; replies to a human still `@` the human; a silent turn stays `NO_REPLY`. Previously a bot replying to a bot omitted the `@` and the reply was silently never delivered.

Real environment tested: a live Feishu group containing two bots and one human participant; the agent ran as one bot and replied across multiple turns to the other bot and to the human. (Identifiers below are redacted.)

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the agent bot in the live group and drove a multi-turn bot-to-bot interview-style exchange; captured the agent session trajectory (inbound prompt + raw model output per turn) and compared it against the messages actually delivered to the group.

Evidence after fix:

- On turns where the sender was the other bot, the inbound prompt carried `[System: "<name>" (open_id: ou_<bot-sender-redacted>) is a bot and receives your reply only if you @mention it. You MUST @mention it in your reply.]`, and the model's reply led with `<at user_id="ou_<bot-sender-redacted>"><name></at> ...` — the bot sender was `@mentioned`.
- On the turn that addressed the human instead, the reply led with `<at user_id="ou_<human-redacted>"><name></at>` (correct target); one turn that needed no reply emitted `NO_REPLY`.
- `ensureMention` backstop verified live: on a turn where the raw model output omitted the bot sender's `@` (it only `@mentioned` the human), the message actually delivered to the group still contained the bot sender's `<at user_id="ou_<bot-sender-redacted>">` — the dispatcher outbound layer backfilled it, so the reply was not silently lost.
- `message_tool` gap reproduced live: with `visibleReplies = message_tool`, a reply that omitted the `@` was delivered without it and the target bot did not receive it (silent drop) — confirming the `channel.ts` path is not yet covered by `ensureMention`.

Observed result after fix: the other bot received and continued the conversation across turns; no silent drop. Replies addressed exactly the intended target and stayed inline (not in a topic thread).

What was not tested: nothing additional for the dispatcher (`automatic`) path — prompt guidance, the `ensureMention` backfill, and inline delivery were all verified live (above) and in `outbound-mention.test.ts`. The `message_tool` path's gap noted above is not addressed in this change and is left as follow-up.
target.author: geekymax
